content:
While reading an email, the "date" is display to the right of the senders emails address.

  • Emails received between 1 and 7 days ago display only the week day, eg Tue
  • Emails between a week and one year old dispay the day and month, eg 10 Apr
  • Emails greater than a year old display the full date.

Tapping the sender email / date line presents a box containing the email's full date and time.

content:
Hi George

Yes there's no problem using both desktop and mobile versions. They can co-exist. Imap is designed to sync multiple clients.

The mobile app asking you to sign-in to your google account is normal. This is the oAuth 2.0 authentication mechanism, a modern and more secure sign-in method for email apps. Many email providers now require this method which replaces traditional user name and password sign-in by apps.

When you sign-in to google, you'll be prompted to give permission, allowing Thunderbird for Android access to you're emails. Once granted, TBA receives an oAuth authentication token from google which it uses to access your emails, rather than your user name and password.
creator: platform34

content:

In order for Thunderbird for Android to successfully sign-in using Imap + oAuth authentication, you need to register the app in Microsoft Entra and grant the users account permission to use Imap / smtp.

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/client-developer/legacy-protocols/how-to-authenticate-an-imap-pop-smtp-application-by-using-oauth

Please advise if your use case is different.

content:
Hi @willy6560

The layout you describe is currently not supported in Thunderbird for Android

Split screen mode can be enabled; listing emails to the left and email content on the right. Settings > general settings > display > Show split screen

The mobile and desktop applications have different heritages and can't be compared. K9-Mail was the foundation app from which Thunderbird for Android evolves and grows. It's code base was written before tablets were common place and doesn't yet offer some features associated with larger screens.

Feel free to add your layout customisation ideas and suggestions on Mozilla connect, where the community can vote for them. The TB team take these into consideration when planning feature additions and roadmaps.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android/tab/most-kudoed

content:
Hi Brian

You can see a count by adding the Thunderbird "Unread Count" widget to your home screen.

The sync process checks for new emails every 15 minutes. It's value is configured in settings > account name > fetching mail > folder poll frequency. 15 minutes is the lowest value allowed by google for background processes and can't be reduced.

For immediate email delivery you should enable the push service. See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

Secondly, you should allow the app to run in the background and exclude it from Android battery optimisations.

  1. Open your android settings > apps > Thunderbird for android > battery usage
  2. Enable "Allow background activity"

content:
I queries an AI which reports the following.

A mail provider can still deliver a spam message to your inbox while blocking the same message when you resend it because those are two different mail flows with different trust signals and filters.

Why this happens

Inbox delivery and outbound sending are judged separately. Incoming mail is scored as a message from the original sender, while forwarding through your SMTP client makes you the sender or at least makes your server the sending source, so it gets re-evaluated under different rules.

Authentication can break on resend. A forwarded or resent message may fail SPF, DKIM, or DMARC alignment because the message now comes from a different server or has changed headers, which can cause the destination to reject or spam it.

Your provider may tolerate incoming spam but block outgoing abuse. Providers often allow suspected spam into junk or even inbox as part of their filtering, but they may aggressively block outbound messages that look spoofed, bulk-like, or policy-violating.

The message content and headers may be enough to trip outbound filters. Forwarding preserves suspicious content, links, and header patterns, so the resend can look more like spam than the original inbound delivery did.

Common example

If you receive a phishing email, your provider may still place it in inbox or junk because it arrived as an incoming message from the apparent sender. But when you forward it via SMTP, the recipient’s mail system sees a new message sent from your account or server, notices authentication mismatch or suspicious headers, and blocks it.

content:
The Unified account is normally automatically enabled when multiple accounts are present. You can toggle the feature on/off from: Settings > General Settings > Display > Show Unified Inbox.

Once enabled, the Unified account is displayed at the top of the account list.

  1. Press the top left menu button or swipe right on the message list.
  2. Tap the down/up arrow (top left of screen) to toggle between the account list and account folder view.

If you have custom imap folder and wish it's content to display in the Unified Inbox ...

  1. Press the top left menu button.
  2. Select an account with it's folder list visible (inbox, draft etc).
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap a folders name.
  5. Toggle "Unify" ON.

Have you managed to return home and test the app function from your home state? Some email providers do indeed block imap/smtp client connections made outwith your normal location or home network.

Have you tried testing Thunderbird Android's Imap/Smtp connection to your mail providers servers? Imap - Settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server Smtp - Settiings > account name > sending mail > outgoing server.

Clicking next on each page will test & validate the connection. Do any errors report when doing so, or prompts to login to your account?

content:
Hi Kev

Assumption your connecting to the server via Imap and not pop.

You should 'Enable 'Sync' on each desired folder. Only Inbox to enabled by default.

  1. Press the top left menu button.
  2. Select your desired account (folders visible).
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap desired folder.
  5. Toggle "Enable Sync" ON.
  6. Repeat for each required folder

content:
Hi Tony

Thunderbird uses Android's own TLS stack and trust store to validate SSL/TLS certificates. You should check you have appropriate and valid dated root certificates in Android's certificate store for lets encrypt chain of trust validation . I believed the main root certificates are ISRG Root X1 and ISRG Root X2.

What version of Android are you running Thunderbird on?
